records.ety: [OE. sepulcre, OF. sepulcre, F. spulcre, fr. L. sepulcrum, sepulchrum, fr. sepelire to bury.]

defs: The place in which the dead body of a human being is interred, or a place set apart for that purpose; a grave; a tomb.
quotes: The stony entrance of this sepulcher.
The first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet dark, unto the sepulcher.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sepulchered (?) or Sepulchred (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Sepulchering (?) or Sepulchring (<?/).]

defs: To bury; to inter; to entomb; as, obscurely sepulchered.
quotes: And so sepulchered in such pomp dost lie That kings for such a tomb would wish to die.
